title: armeria pseudarmeria subsp. castellana
intro: Armeria pseudarmeria subsp. castellana is a subspecies of the Armeria plant, commonly known as the Castilian thrift or Castilian sea-pink, which is a perennial plant native to the Iberian Peninsula. The medicinal properties of Armeria pseudarmeria subsp. castellana include its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which have been used to treat respiratory issues and coughs. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of bronchitis and asthma, making it a valuable remedy for those suffering from respiratory problems. Armeria pseudarmeria subsp. castellana is often used in herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
